claim_text: The official MCP Registry launched in preview on 2025-09-08 and entered an API freeze (v0.1) on 2025-10-24, giving integrators a stable interface ahead of GA.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The official MCP Registry is not intended for direct host-application consumption; it is a primary source of truth that downstream sub-registries (Smithery, Glama, GitHub, Anthropic) consume via its OpenAPI spec.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: MCP Registry namespace ownership is verified by reverse-DNS naming: io.github.* via GitHub OAuth/OIDC and com.example.* via DNS TXT or HTTP challenge, so only the legitimate account/domain owner can publish under a namespace.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: A Claude Code plugin marketplace is a git repository containing a .claude-plugin/marketplace.json manifest that lists plugins; users add it with /plugin marketplace add owner/repo and install individual plugins from it.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Anthropic operates two plugin marketplaces: claude-plugins-official (curated at Anthropic's discretion, no public application) and claude-community (anthropics/claude-plugins-community, an automated review+safety pipeline whose approved entries are pinned to a commit SHA and synced nightly).
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Claude Code exposes no central programmatic plugin-search API; plugin discovery is git-manifest based (marketplace.json fetched from configured marketplaces), declared in settings.json via extraKnownMarketplaces/strictKnownMarketplaces.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: vercel-labs/skills uses GitHub as the registry: any public repository with a root SKILL.md is installable via npx skills add <owner/repo>, with .skills.json + skills-lock.json mirroring package.json/lockfile semantics.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The skills.sh leaderboard ranks agent skills by total install count, which the Vercel ecosystem treats as the primary popularity/quality signal.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Smithery's registry exposes a REST API (GET /servers, Bearer token) whose server records carry a verified boolean, a useCount, an isDeployed flag, and a relevance score, supporting full-text and semantic search.
label: PROBABLY TRUE

claim_text: Smithery is the largest public MCP registry (roughly 5,600-6,000 servers in early 2026), an order of magnitude larger than the official MCP Registry at that time.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Public popularity signals (GitHub stars, Smithery install/use counts) are weak proxies for MCP server reliability: in a 100-server stress test the most-starred server ranked 41st by pass rate, and download counts measure ecosystem interest rather than runtime success.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Fake GitHub stars are widespread and cheaply purchased (roughly 4.5-6 million suspected fake stars across ~15,835 repositories; market prices ~$0.10-$1.62 per star), so a raw star count can be inflated by orders of magnitude.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: StarScout detects suspected fake-star campaigns by the divergence between anomalous star spikes and genuine usage signals — the methodology the recommender's fake-star gate reuses.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The first confirmed malicious MCP server found in the wild was postmark-mcp (September 2025), which silently added a BCC recipient to every email sent by agents using it, exfiltrating internal mail.
label: POSSIBLY TRUE

claim_text: MCP tool poisoning is a form of indirect prompt injection via attacker-controlled tool metadata (descriptions/schemas), with measured attack success rates up to ~73% on some models; OWASP classifies it as MCP03 and it is recognized in NSA guidance.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Multiple real MCP supply-chain incidents occurred in 2025-2026, including the October 2025 Smithery hosting supply-chain breach, the mcp-remote command-injection RCE, and the MCP Inspector RCE (CVE-2025-49596).
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: For npm-distributed MCP servers, the available provenance mechanism is npm package provenance — SLSA Build Level 2 attestations signed via Sigstore (Fulcio short-lived certs) and recorded in the Rekor transparency log, verifiable with npm audit signatures before install.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Build provenance attests where/how an artifact was built but not that it is safe: attackers using stolen maintainer credentials and typosquatting can produce artifacts whose trust signals still validate, so provenance is a necessary-not-sufficient input to a trust grade.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: GitHub is the de facto base registry for the whole ecosystem (skills install straight from repos; marketplaces and the MCP registry resolve to repos), and the strongest cheap legitimacy signal GitHub itself recommends is official-org membership combined with star count.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Anthropic's official plugin marketplace ships an explicit trust disclaimer — it cannot verify that plugins work as intended or that they will not change — which establishes that a recommender must propose, never auto-install, and must surface trust signals rather than imply endorsement.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Registry-level safety in this ecosystem is immature: the official MCP Registry delegates security scanning to package registries and relies on manual takedown, and there is no conformance test suite yet (only a roadmap commitment).
label: POSSIBLY TRUE

claim_text: The official MCP Registry is production-ready for direct client integration.
label: DOUBTFUL
